ai-slop.description: Deterministic rules for generated-code residue and maintainability shortcuts.
ai-slop.name: AI Slop

reasons: functions are small and low complexity

metric_definitions.activity_score: File revision_count divided by max revision_count across files in this output.
metric_definitions.aislop: AI slop detection results from aislop. Contains a 0-100 score, engine summaries, and per-file diagnostics detecting patterns typical of AI-generated code such as trivial comments, swallowed exceptions, generic naming, and bare excepts. Diagnostics include analysisSource to distinguish deterministic_static_analysis findings from opt-in llm_review findings.
metric_definitions.avg_function_complexity: Average cyclomatic complexity among functions Lizard reported for this file.
metric_definitions.avg_function_nloc: Average non-comment lines of code among functions Lizard reported for this file.
metric_definitions.avg_nesting_depth: Average function max_nesting_depth for a file.
metric_definitions.blank_line_count: Blank lines for this file from cloc.
metric_definitions.boolean_operator_count: Number of boolean operators inside branch conditions. Maps to CodeScene's Complex Conditional signal.
metric_definitions.class_count: Number of Python classes in a file. Early module responsibility pressure signal related to Brain Class/God Class analysis.
metric_definitions.classic_smells: Classic code, architectural, and structural smell findings. Preserved as raw evidence under the top-level classic_smells key and not currently used in health scoring.
metric_definitions.code_duplication_ratio: Duplicated code lines as a percentage of total code lines.
metric_definitions.code_duplication_total: Number of duplicated code lines across all files. A line is duplicated when it belongs to a block of 10 or more consecutive non-empty, non-comment lines that appear identically in at least one other file. Uses union of overlapping ranges per file.
metric_definitions.code_line_count: Physical code lines for this file from cloc.
metric_definitions.comment_line_count: Comment lines for this file from cloc.
metric_definitions.complex_conditional_count: Number of conditionals with boolean operators or chained comparisons. Maps to CodeScene's Complex Conditional signal.
metric_definitions.conditional_count: Number of conditional branch expressions observed in functions by the Python AST structural analyzer.
metric_definitions.conditional_penalty: Function health penalty from AST complex_conditional_count.
metric_definitions.dependency_analysis: File-level dependency pressure derived from local Python import edges.
metric_definitions.dependency_score: Reserved for a future score based on dependency pressure.
metric_definitions.file_duplication_ratio: Percentage of files with byte-identical contents to at least one other file.
metric_definitions.file_duplication_total: Number of files with byte-identical contents to at least one other file in the project.
metric_definitions.file_size_health: Fallback file-level size health used when Lizard reports no functions for a file.
metric_definitions.folders_total: Total number of non-empty directories in the project.
metric_definitions.function_count: Number of functions Lizard reported for this file.
metric_definitions.health: Prototype static maintainability score. Scale is 0.0 to 10.0, where higher is healthier. Derived from function size, complexity, parameters, nesting, conditionals, and import coupling. Appears as the 'score' field inside metrics.scores.health at every level (function, file, directory, repo). Same value as health_score.
metric_definitions.health_score: Alias for health. Same 0-10 static maintainability score. Used in some contexts as a shorthand; the underlying value is identical to health.
metric_definitions.high_complexity_function_count: Number of functions with cyclomatic complexity greater than or equal to the configured high-complexity threshold.
metric_definitions.hotspot_activity_weight: Initial hotspot activity signal. Currently equal to revision_count.
metric_definitions.hotspot_activity_weight_normalized: hotspot_activity_weight divided by the maximum hotspot_activity_weight observed in this analysis output.
metric_definitions.hotspot_score: Transparent file-level hotspot score. On file nodes: revision_count_normalized * hotspot_size_score. In the top-level hotspots list: activity_score * size_score, where those values are the same normalized factors.
metric_definitions.hotspot_score_formula: The formula used for hotspot_score on this node.
metric_definitions.hotspot_size_score: File code_line_count divided by max code_line_count across files in this output.
metric_definitions.huge_cyclomatic_complexity_ratio: Percentage of functions with cyclomatic complexity at or above the high-complexity threshold.
metric_definitions.huge_depth_ratio: Percentage of functions whose maximum nesting depth exceeds the configurable huge_depth threshold.
metric_definitions.huge_depth_total: Number of functions whose maximum nesting depth exceeds the configurable huge_depth threshold.
metric_definitions.huge_folder_ratio: Percentage of directories where the count of immediate children exceeds the configurable huge_folder threshold.
metric_definitions.huge_folder_total: Number of directories where the count of immediate children (files + subdirectories) exceeds the configurable huge_folder threshold (default: 50).
metric_definitions.huge_method_ratio: Percentage of functions with nloc at or above the large-function threshold.
metric_definitions.huge_non_headerfile_ratio: Percentage of source files whose code line count exceeds the configurable huge_file threshold.
metric_definitions.huge_non_headerfile_total: Number of source files whose code line count exceeds the configurable huge_file threshold (default: 2000).
metric_definitions.import_coupling_count: Sum of outgoing_dependency_count and incoming_dependency_count for this file.
metric_definitions.import_coupling_penalty: File health penalty applied from import_coupling_count.
metric_definitions.incoming_dependency_count: Number of unique local Python files that import this file.
metric_definitions.large_function_count: Number of functions with nloc greater than or equal to the configured large-function threshold.
metric_definitions.lines_per_file: Average code lines per file: total_code_line_count / file_count.
metric_definitions.low_health_file_count: Number of descendant files below the low-health threshold.
metric_definitions.max_function_complexity: Maximum cyclomatic complexity among functions Lizard reported for this file.
metric_definitions.max_function_nloc: Maximum non-comment lines of code among functions Lizard reported for this file.
metric_definitions.max_function_parameter_count: Maximum parameter count among functions Lizard reported for this file.
metric_definitions.max_nesting_depth: Maximum nested control-flow depth observed by the Python AST structural analyzer. Maps to CodeScene's Nested Complexity signal.
metric_definitions.median_child_health_score: Median descendant file health score in a directory or repository rollup.
metric_definitions.methods_total: Total number of functions across all source files in the project.
metric_definitions.nesting_penalty: Function health penalty from AST max_nesting_depth.
metric_definitions.outgoing_dependency_count: Number of unique local Python files imported by this file.
metric_definitions.revision_count: Number of Git revisions that touched this repository-relative path during the configured analysis window. Produced by Code Maat revisions analysis.
metric_definitions.revision_count_normalized: revision_count divided by the maximum revision_count observed in this analysis output. Range: 0.0 to 1.0.
metric_definitions.risk_score: Reserved for a future score combining temporal activity with low health.
metric_definitions.scores: Derived score families. Raw extracted metrics remain available outside this object.
metric_definitions.size_score: File code_line_count divided by max code_line_count across files in this output.
metric_definitions.static_analysis: File-level rollups derived from function metrics extracted by Lizard.
metric_definitions.structural_erosion: Fraction of the codebase's total complexity mass residing in high-complexity functions (CC > high-complexity threshold). Mass per function is CC * sqrt(nloc). Range: 0.0 to 1.0.
metric_definitions.structural_metrics: Function-level AST metrics that approximate CodeScene-style nested complexity and complex conditional findings.
metric_definitions.top_level_function_count: Number of top-level Python functions in a file.
metric_definitions.top_low_health_files: Lowest-scoring descendant file paths, capped for readability.
metric_definitions.total_cyclomatic_complexity: Sum of cyclomatic complexity values across all functions in the project.
metric_definitions.total_method_lines: Total non-comment lines of code across all function bodies in the project.
metric_definitions.total_raw_lines: Total physical line count across all source files, including code, comment, and blank lines.
metric_definitions.warning_suppression_kloc: Warning suppression count per 1000 code lines. Set to 0 if total code lines < 1000.
metric_definitions.warning_suppression_total: Number of warning suppression comments across all Python source files. Matches pylint:, noqa, type: ignore, pyright: ignore, and nosec patterns.
metric_definitions.weighted_average_file_health_score: Directory/repository code-line-weighted average of file health scores.
metric_definitions.weighted_average_function_health: File-level nloc-weighted average of function health scores.
metric_definitions.worst_child_health_score: Lowest descendant file health score in a directory or repository rollup.
metric_definitions.worst_function_cap: File-level cap equal to worst_function_health + worst_function_cap_bonus.
metric_definitions.worst_function_cap_bonus: Configurable file-level health cap bonus added to worst_function_health.
metric_definitions.worst_function_health: Lowest function health score observed in a file.

schema_notes.path_kind: All node paths and dependency paths are repository-relative POSIX paths unless explicitly documented otherwise.
